name: Blueberry Lattice Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 pint Blueberries; rinsed, drained and picked over
1 tsp Sugar for finishing the top of the pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare and chill the dough.
2. To make the filling, combine 1 cup of the blueberries with the sugar in a nonreactive saucepan. Bring to a simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar is melted and the mixture is very liquid (about 5 minutes).
3. Combine the cornstarch and water in a small bowl and whisk into the blueberry and sugar mixture. Return everything to the pan and cook, stirring constantly, over low heat until the mixture comes to a boil, thickens and becomes clear.
4. If it does not become clear, continue to cook over low heat for a few more minutes until it does. Pour into a large bowl and stir in the remaining filling ingredients, except the blueberries, then add the remaining 3 cups blueberries.
5. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Set a rack at the upper and lower thirds of the oven.
6. Roll out the bottom crust and arrange in the pan. Pour the cooled filling into the bottom crust.
7. Prepare a lattice top crust. Flute the edge of the pie and carefully brush with egg wash. If desired, sprinkle the top of the pie with sugar.
8. Place the pie in the oven on the lower rack. Reduce the temperature to 375 degrees. Bake for about 40 minutes, until the crust is baked through and a deep golden brown and the filling is gently bubbling.
9. If the top crust has not colored sufficiently after 30 minutes of baking, move the pie to the upper rack of the oven for the last 10 minutes. Cool on a rack.
Yield: one 9-inch pie
Variation: Top with Almond Crumble topping.
